name: standards-refresh.sh crawl stops following links past the entry's max_depth bound so deep chains never contribute rules
command:
OUT=/tmp/cl564-r36-$$; mkdir -p $OUT/docs $OUT/out && printf '

D0

Depth zero guidance rule

The seed page carries the first guidance statement of the chain.

next' > $OUT/docs/index.html && printf '

D1

Depth one guidance rule

The first hop carries the second guidance statement of the chain.

next' > $OUT/docs/p1.html && printf '

D2

Depth two guidance rule

The second hop carries the third guidance statement of the chain.

next' > $OUT/docs/p2.html && printf '

D3

Depth three guidance rule

The third hop is past the bound and must never contribute.

' > $OUT/docs/p3.html && cat > $OUT/reg.yaml <e.err; T=$OUT/out/node/r36-src.yaml; grep -q 'pages_fetched=3' e.err && grep -q 'depth-two-guidance-rule' $T && ! grep -q 'depth-three-guidance-rule' $T && echo max-depth-bound-honored 1>&2; rm -rf $OUT
